title: SeedSmith: LLM-Driven Seed Synthesis for Directed Fuzzing

title: The AI Assessment Dilemma: How Universities Are Adapting to Generative Tech
snippet: As generative AI tools become deeply integrated into student workflows, universities are shifting away from AI detection software toward redesigned assessments. Peer institutions are implementing oral exams, portfolio-based grading, and supervised writing sessions to ensure academic integrity.
match_reason: websearch (news_article) — This article directly addresses academic integrity, assessment redesign, and AI policy challenges at peer research universities.

title: Bridging the Digital Divide: Equity and Access in University AI Initiatives
snippet: A new report highlights how unequal access to premium generative AI subscriptions is creating a secondary digital divide among college students. The study urges public university systems to provide subsidized or free institutional access to ensure equity in learning environments.
match_reason: websearch (news_article) — It directly addresses AI access and equity for students, which is a core component of the committee's mandate.

title: UC Berkeley Launches New Guidelines for Generative AI in Research and Teaching
snippet: UC Berkeley has released an updated framework for the ethical use of generative AI in academic research and undergraduate classrooms. The guidelines focus on data privacy, intellectual property protection, and maintaining human-in-the-loop oversight for grading and research methodologies.
match_reason: websearch (press_release) — This is a major policy and governance update from a flagship University of California campus regarding AI in teaching and research.

title: AI in the Classroom: A Multi-University Study on Student Learning Outcomes
snippet: A collaborative study involving several R1 research universities examines the impact of generative AI tutors on student learning outcomes. The findings suggest that while AI tools improve initial comprehension, they can reduce long-term retention if not paired with active learning strategies.
match_reason: websearch (publication) — This is a major study on AI use in higher education teaching and learning, directly relevant to the committee's academic focus.

title: GaP: A Graph-as-Policy Multi-Agent Self-Learning Harness For Variational Automation Tasks
snippet: For robots to work reliably in commercial and industrial applications, can recent advances in agentic coding systems combine interpretable robot programming with the open-world adaptability of model-free policies? We introduce Graph-as-Policy (GaP), a multi-agent coding harness that generates directed computation graphs with perception, planning, and control nodes.

title: An Exploratory Study of Interpretation Difficulty in Cybercrime-Related Discord Chats
snippet: This paper presents an exploratory study of interpretation difficulty in Discord chats related to cybercrime. We construct reference interpretations of purposefully selected difficult messages, which were reviewed by an expert. We then use them to evaluate human and large language model (LLM) interpretations under different context conditions.

title: UChicago Law School Releases Sweeping AI Pedagogy Memo Banning Electronics and Mandating In-Person Exams
snippet: The University of Chicago Law School released a memo detailing sweeping policy overhauls to transform its pedagogy in response to generative AI. The school plans to ban electronics from first-year classrooms and administer all first-year exams in-person, urging upper-level courses to follow.

title: UK Study Finds Evidence of 'Conscientious Objectors' and AI Backlash Among University Students
snippet: A new study of students across seven UK universities found that 32% are 'conscientious objectors' who do not use AI for their studies due to ethical concerns and threats to their future. The report highlights that universal AI usage cannot be assumed and warns of a growing student backlash.

title: California State University Systemwide AI Strategy Focuses on Preventing a Digital Divide
